title: SocioTechnical Constitution Runtime — Complete Component Specification
scope: Full specification of the runtime enforcement layer and all supporting components for the SocioTechnical Constitution, including: Contract Window, Policy Engine, Evidence Observatory, Constitutional Council Dashboard, Amendment Process UI, Break-Glass Override, Role-Based Access, Integration Adaptors, and four diagrams.
primary_objective: Provide an implementable, zero-translation blueprint for the entire runtime.
not_claimed: Integration with specific enterprise tools beyond abstract adaptors

constraints: All components must obey the Constitution's principles, invariant set, and progressive enforcement model
known_gaps: No runtime code — all specifications are declarative

risk_classification.external_action_scope: Blocks merges, deployments, or AI actions only after progressive enforcement

acceptance_criteria.description: Loads a valid contract without errors
Fails loading with clear message if schema violated
Write updates are atomic (write to temp, rename)
validate catches missing required fields
All Article VIII invariants implemented as testable rules
Halt matrix entries can be triggered by simulated events
Rule evaluation completes within 200ms for up to 50 rules
WARN decision does not block but flags in V&T
All gate evaluations are logged immutably
Query returns evidence for specific contract and time range
Logging fails gracefully if disk full, alert is raised
Logs include enough detail to reconstruct the decision for audit
Strip renders within 100ms of contract load
Strip color matches highest-severity halt condition
Kanban cards accurately reflect AC status from contract
Dragging a card updates the underlying contract file
Halt matrix shows all active halts with resolution links
V&T detail shows all four fields per AC
Challenge/Repair buttons only visible to authorized roles
Contract Window live-reloads when contract file changes on disk
AI assistant cannot trigger merge or deploy actions even if instructed
SRE can execute a deploy during approved window with valid credential
Break-glass requires dual approval for high-risk services
Break-glass cannot be used for roles without authorization
Override correctly bypasses specified halt conditions
Override auto-expires and is logged
All override events appear in Evidence Observatory and Council dashboard
Dashboard displays active overrides within 10 seconds of occurrence
Amendment voting works and results are recorded in amendment log
Only Council members can access
Amendments can be proposed, diffed, and submitted
Voting follows the Council's defined quorum
Approved amendments are reflected in the runtime immediately
Git hook blocks push when policy demands, with clear message
CI plugin fails the build if gates not met
LLM gateway adds V&T metadata to flagged responses
All components communicate via defined APIs/events
The system can run entirely from a local folder without external services
The Constitution's own V&T is tracked within the Evidence Observatory

halt_conditions.trigger: Active build contract not found in contracts/active/
Any Article VIII invariant broken during operation
halt_conditions.resolution: Fix the violating artifact; clear halt

halt_conditions.trigger: Role attempts action outside allowed scope
Disk space for evidence logs exhausted

vnt_statement.what: Complete build contract for the SocioTechnical Constitution runtime, including UI, enforcement, evidence, council, amendment, and integration components, plus four diagrams.
vnt_statement.true: All components are specified with data models, acceptance criteria, and halt behavior. Four diagrams are present.
vnt_statement.assumed: The implementing team has access to the defined schemas and the Sociotechnical Constitution document.
vnt_statement.uncertain: Exact choice of UI library or language may shift; the spec is abstract enough to accommodate.
vnt_statement.unverified: Whether the system as specified will achieve the intended governance outcomes without quantitative validation.
